Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Below is a singlestatement DuckDB query that fulfils every requirement Replace K with the desired number of top variants

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: SELECT case_id LISTAGG activity ORDER BY timestamp AS activity_seq FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Count how many cases follow each distinct variant

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: SELECT activity_seq COUNT AS case_cnt FROM ordered_seq GROUP BY activity_seq

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Determine the topK variants by frequency

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: SELECT activity_seq FROM variant_counts ORDER BY case_cnt DESC LIMIT K

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Keep only cases that belong to those topK variants

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: SELECT DISTINCT case_id FROM ordered_seq WHERE activity_seq IN SELECT activity_seq FROM top_k_variants

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Return all original events that belong to the selected cases

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: SELECT el case_id el activity el timestamp FROM event_log AS el JOIN cases_in_top_k AS c ON el case_id equals c case_id ORDER BY el case_id el timestamp

Name: Validation - PE
Text: How the query satisfies the specification Builds the ordered activity sequence for each case_id Groups the sequences and counts how many cases share each exact variant Picks the K most frequent variants Identifies the case_ids that belong to those topK variants Returns all original rows events from the selected cases preserving the original order
